heading: State grants for projects regarding traumatic brain injury
label: 300d–52 State grants for projects regarding traumatic brain injury

Prior to amendment, text read as follows: “For purposes of this section, the term ‘traumatic brain injury’ means an acquired injury to the brain. Such term does not include brain dysfunction caused by congenital or degenerative disorders, nor birth trauma, but may include brain injuries caused by anoxia due to trauma. The Secretary may revise the definition of such term as the Secretary determines necessary, after consultation with States and other appropriate public or nonprofit private entities.”Pub. children.heading: In general
children.label: a In general
children.text: The Secretary, acting through the Administrator for the Administration for Community Living, may make grants to States and American Indian consortia for the purpose of carrying out projects to improve access to rehabilitation and other services regarding traumatic brain injury.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: The Secretary may make a grant under subsection (a) only if the State or American Indian consortium involved agrees to establish an advisory board within the appropriate health department of the State or American Indian consortium or within another department as designated by the chief executive officer of the State or American Indian consortium.

children.heading: Functions
children.label: 2 Functions
children.text: An advisory board established under paragraph (1) shall advise and make recommendations to the State or American Indian consortium on ways to improve services coordination regarding traumatic brain injury. Such advisory boards shall encourage citizen participation through the establishment of public hearings and other types of community outreach programs. In developing recommendations under this paragraph, such boards shall consult with Federal, State, and local governmental agencies and with citizens groups and other private entities.

children.label: i
children.text: the corresponding State or American Indian consortium agencies involved;

children.label: ii
children.text: public and nonprofit private health related organizations;

children.label: iii
children.text: other disability advisory or planning groups within the State or American Indian consortium;

children.label: iv
children.text: members of an organization or foundation representing individuals with traumatic brain injury in that State or American Indian consortium; and

children.label: v
children.text: injury control programs at the State or local level if such programs exist; and

children.label: B
children.text: a substantial number of individuals with traumatic brain injury, or the family members of such individuals.

children.heading: In general
children.label: 1 In general
children.text: With respect to the costs to be incurred by a State or American Indian consortium in carrying out the purpose described in subsection (a), the Secretary may make a grant under such subsection only if the State or American Indian consortium agrees to make available non-Federal contributions toward such costs in an amount that is not less than $1 for each $2 of Federal funds provided under the grant.

children.heading: Determination of amount contributed
children.label: 2 Determination of amount contributed
children.text: Non-Federal contributions under paragraph (1) may be in cash or in kind, fairly evaluated, including plant, equipment, or services. Amounts provided by the Federal Government, or services assisted or subsidized to any significant extent by the Federal Government, may not be included in determining the amount of such contributions.

children.heading: Application for grant
children.label: d Application for grant
children.text: The Secretary may make a grant under subsection (a) only if an application for the grant is submitted to the Secretary and the application is in such form, is made in such manner, and contains such agreements, assurances, and information as the Secretary determines to be necessary to carry out this section.

children.heading: Use of State and American Indian consortium grants
children.label: e Use of State and American Indian consortium grants
i
children.text: shall promote full participation by individuals with traumatic brain injury and their families in decision making regarding the services and supports; and

children.label: ii
children.text: shall be designed for children, youth, and adults with traumatic brain injury.

children.label: B
children.text: To focus on outreach to underserved and inappropriately served individuals, such as individuals in institutional settings, individuals with low socioeconomic resources, individuals in rural communities, and individuals in culturally and linguistically diverse communities.

children.label: C
children.text: To award contracts to nonprofit entities for consumer or family service access training, consumer support, peer mentoring, and parent to parent programs.

children.label: D
children.text: To develop individual and family service coordination or case management systems.

children.label: E
children.text: To support other needs identified by the advisory board under subsection (b) for the State or American Indian consortium involved.

children.heading: In general
children.label: A In general
children.text: State or American Indian consortium services and supports provided under a grant under this section shall reflect the best practices in the field of traumatic brain injury, shall be in compliance with title II of the Americans with Disabilities Act of 1990 [ et seq.], and shall be supported by quality assurance measures as well as state-of-the-art health care and integrated community supports, regardless of the severity of injury.42 U.S.C. 12131

children.heading: Demonstration by State agency
children.label: B Demonstration by State agency
children.text: The State or American Indian consortium agency responsible for administering amounts received under a grant under this section shall demonstrate that it has obtained knowledge and expertise of traumatic brain injury and the unique needs associated with traumatic brain injury.

children.label: A
children.text: educate consumers and families;

children.label: B
children.text: train professionals in public and private sector financing (such as third party payers, State agencies, community-based providers, schools, and educators);

children.label: C
children.text: develop or improve case management or service coordination systems;

children.label: D
children.text: develop best practices in areas such as family or consumer support, return to work, housing or supportive living personal assistance services, assistive technology and devices, behavioral health services, substance abuse services, and traumatic brain injury treatment and rehabilitation;

children.label: E
children.text: tailor existing State or American Indian consortium systems to provide accommodations to the needs of individuals with traumatic brain injury (including systems administered by the State or American Indian consortium departments responsible for health, mental health, labor/employment, education, intellectual disabilities or developmental disorders, transportation, and correctional systems);

children.label: F
children.text: improve data sets coordinated across systems and other needs identified by a State or American Indian consortium plan supported by its advisory council; and

children.label: G
children.text: develop capacity within targeted communities.

children.heading: Coordination of activities
children.label: f Coordination of activities
children.text: The Secretary shall ensure that activities under this section are coordinated as appropriate with other Federal agencies that carry out activities regarding traumatic brain injury.

children.heading: Report
children.label: g Report
children.text: Not less than biennially, the Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Energy and Commerce of the House of Representatives, and to the Committee on Health, Education, Labor, and Pensions of the Senate, a report describing the findings and results of the programs established under this section and , including measures of outcomes and consumer and surrogate satisfaction.section 300d–53 of this title
The terms “American Indian consortium” and “State” have the meanings given to those terms in .section 300d–53 of this title

The term “traumatic brain injury” means an acquired injury to the brain. Such term does not include brain dysfunction caused by congenital or degenerative disorders, nor birth trauma, but may include brain injuries caused by anoxia due to trauma. The Secretary may revise the definition of such term as the Secretary determines necessary, after consultation with States and other appropriate public or nonprofit private entities.

children.heading: Authorization of appropriations
children.label: i Authorization of appropriations
children.text: For the purpose of carrying out this section, there are authorized to be appropriated $7,321,000 for each of fiscal years 2020 through 2024.
